Application.Name: FanBridge
Application.Overview:
FanBridge – bridge Unraid drive temperatures to an external microcontroller (RP2040) over USB serial to control PWM fans.
USB Passthrough: To allow the container to detect your FanBridge controller(s), you must map the USB device. - Option 1 (Recommended): Enable "Privileged" mode. This allows FanBridge to automatically scan and detect all plugged-in controllers. - Option 2: Add a "Device" mapping for your specific controller's serial port (e.g., /dev/ttyACM0 or /dev/serial/by-id/usb-...).
Defaults match a known‑working Unraid setup: port 8080, appdata at /mnt/maincache/appdata/fanbridge, the Unraid emhttp directory mounted read‑only at /unraid.

Config.Name: AppData (config)
Config.Description: Container configuration directory. Persist settings and user data here.

Config.Name: USB Controller Device
Config.Description: Pass through the USB controller serial device if not running in Privileged mode.

Config.Name: USB Bus (for FW updates)
Config.Description: Required for RP2040 firmware updates. The controller changes USB identity during BOOTSEL; this mapping lets the container detect it.

Config.Name: Disk by-label (for FW updates)
Config.Description: Helps the container locate the RPI-RP2 boot volume by label during firmware updates.
